cards.title: The Cases That Broke 2024
cards.caption: A&E dropped their most-viewed Cold Case Files moments of the year in one compilation. The internet's true-crime obsession, quantified. Which case still haunts you? Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.povText: POV: it's 2am and you swore you'd sleep after one more case

cards.title: The Voice That Started True Crime
cards.caption: Robert Stack. That trench coat. That music. Unsolved Mysteries basically invented the genre everyone's addicted to now. Full episodes are back. Did this show raise you? Found via Threadverse.
cards.povText: POV: this theme song still makes the hair on your neck stand up

cards.title: Cold for 20 Years. One Clue Cracked It.
cards.caption: Murders that sat unsolved for decades—until a single piece of DNA changed everything. The compilation of clues that finally caught them. What's the wildest cold-case break you've heard? Via Threadverse.
cards.quote: For decades, these murder cases went unsolved—until one critical clue changed everything.

cards.title: The True Crime Book Fans Keep Recommending
cards.caption: A quiet Bluesky rec for Cold Case Chronicles—real stories of the missing, the murdered, and the vanished-by-choice. Low reach, niche book plug. Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: Excellent read for true crime fans.

cards.title: 5 Cases. Every One Ends Insane.
cards.caption: Cold cases where the twist is the whole story—solved years later with endings nobody saw coming. Which twist wrecked you the most? Curated by Threadverse.

cards.title: 4 Cold Cases, Solved in 2024
cards.caption: Another cold-case twist compilation—decent thumbnail, thin description, the red arrow does the heavy lifting. Overlaps hard with the stronger cases in this feed. Via Threadverse.

cards.title: Hiding in Plain Sight for 68 Years
cards.caption: A 1956 double homicide—cracked open by DNA in 2024. The killer was hiding in plain sight the whole time. Justice, finally served. How long is too long to wait? Found via Threadverse.
cards.quote: Who was the culprit hiding in plain sight for decades?

cards.title: Caught After 41 Years. By a Smoothie Cup.
cards.caption: Nassau County investigators cracked a 41-year-old case using DNA off a discarded smoothie cup. Your trash can testify. What would YOU refuse to throw away now? Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: Cold Case Solved After 41 Years Using DNA Collected from Smoothie Cup
cards.povText: POV: you finally understand why detectives go through your garbage

cards.title: A Toddler Left At The Crime Scene
cards.caption: Robin Lawrence, a gifted artist, murdered in her own home. Her toddler left to fend for herself. This cold case still haunts. What do you think cracked it open? (surfaced by Threadverse)

cards.title: He Saw Something Odd On London Road
cards.caption: January 1993. A man drives down London Road in Dartford and sees something that doesn't belong. 20 years cold, then solved. The Claire Tiltman case. (found via Threadverse)

cards.title: The Face The Fence Hid
cards.caption: Jennifer Kesse, missing since 1/24/06 from Orlando. There's video of the suspect walking away, face blocked by a fence for exactly 3 frames. Cold cases with video hit different. (via Threadverse)
cards.quote: The driver's face blocked by the fence.
cards.povText: POV: the one clip that could solve it, and a fence is in the way

cards.title: 18 Years. Still No Answer.
cards.caption: The case that gripped a nation for 18 years. The Crime Agents reopen the disappearance of Madeleine McCann. Can a case this cold ever truly close? (surfaced by Threadverse)
cards.quote: the case that has gripped the nation for 18 years

cards.title: 60 Years. Then DNA Talked.
cards.caption: She left them behind. 60 years later, DNA finally revealed why. Another Jane Doe gets her name back. Othram keeps closing the ones everyone gave up on. (found via Threadverse)

cards.title: One Murder Exposed A Serial Killer
cards.caption: Cold Case Files: detectives chase a teen's murderer and uncover a serial killer hiding in plain sight. Season 2, Ep 23. How many were connected? (surfaced by Threadverse)
cards.quote: This murder case exposed a serial killer.

cards.title: Students Cracked A Cold Case
cards.caption: WCU students solved a Durham cold case and gave a grieving family closure. No name, no victim, no detail: too thin to land as its own post.

cards.title: 37 Years. She Left 3 Little Kids.
cards.caption: "My sister-in-law Teri is finally in California's Cold Case Database." Run off the road, chased down, stabbed. 37 years waiting for justice. Family voices hit hardest. (via Threadverse)
cards.quote: 37 years is a long time to wait for justice for her murder.

cards.title: A Bite Mark Exposed the LAPD
cards.caption: Cold for 23 years. Then DNA from a single bite mark pointed to a killer hiding inside the LAPD itself. The Sherri Rasmussen case still gives me chills. Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: a killer hiding in plain sight—within the LAPD

cards.title: The Tylenol Killer Was Never Caught
cards.caption: Someone poisoned Tylenol bottles, put them back on shelves, and walked away. 40+ years later Netflix reopens the case that changed how every pill you take is sealed. Found via Threadverse.

cards.title: Missing Since 1973. Arrest in 2021.
cards.caption: A Dallas cold case sat untouched for 48 years. Then one arrest shook the whole community. Who do you think finally cracked it? Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: One Anonymous Call Named the Killer
cards.caption: A 13-year-old girl went missing. For years, nothing. Then one anonymous phone call led police straight to her killer. Who makes a call like that? Curated by Threadverse.
cards.quote: An anonymous caller leads police to the killer

cards.title: One of the UK's Oldest Cold Cases
cards.caption: Grandmother Louisa Dunne was murdered decades ago. Detectives never stopped hunting. Now they may finally have her killer. Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: one of the UK's oldest cold cases

cards.title: Cold for 40 Years. Then Her Daughter Spoke.
cards.caption: A child abduction case went cold for four decades. An arrest finally came, caught on body cam, as the daughter speaks out. Some cases never really close. Found via Threadverse.
cards.quote: 40-Year Cold Case Child Abduction as Daughter Speaks Out

cards.title: The Shoebox Murders of 1886
cards.caption: An 1886 cold case where the victim's remains were found in a shoebox. Nobody knows who they were or who killed them. Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: He Vanished After a Rainbow Gathering
cards.caption: A young man disappeared after a Rainbow Gathering. His body turned up months later. What happened to Russell? Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: Podcast plug, no payoff
cards.caption: A forensic psychologist breaking down a real cold case sounds great, but this is a bare podcast plug with no hook. Threadverse skipped past it for a reason.

cards.title: An anonymous letter told them where to dig
cards.caption: Police are digging at a new location in a decades-old Dutch cold case, after an anonymous letter pointed them there. Who writes a letter like that 30 years later? Threadverse surfaced it.

cards.title: Book one, action packed
cards.caption: A cold-case mystery novel launch. Fine for a genre shelf, but it's an author selling a book, not a story that pulls you in. Threadverse keeps the best 50 of 5,000, this wasn't it.

cards.title: Acquitted after 30 years — not enough proof
cards.caption: A 71-year-old walks free in a 1992 cold case: the court ruled the evidence wasn't lawful or convincing enough. Justice, or one that got away? Threadverse pulled this from the noise.
cards.povText: POV: the case sat cold for 30 years, then the verdict was 'not enough proof'

cards.title: 39 years later, they finally arrested someone
cards.caption: Lachelle 'Shelly' Jeannine Waite was strangled in her own home. Nearly four decades later, an arrest. Say her name. Threadverse surfaced Shelly's story from 5,000 posts.

cards.title: A deathbed confession solved it — 24 years later
cards.caption: A mother and daughter vanished 24 years ago. A dying man's confession finally led police to the bodies, buried in a neighbor's yard he'd always suspected. Threadverse found this one.
cards.povText: POV: the answer was buried in the yard next door the whole time

cards.title: The Shoebox Murders of 1886
cards.caption: Remains found in a shoebox. A killer never named. The Wallingford Shoebox Murders have stayed cold since 1886 — and the scene was bizarre. Who was the victim? Threadverse surfaced it.

cards.title: Vanished after a Rainbow Gathering
cards.caption: A young man disappears after a Rainbow Gathering, body found months later. Intriguing premise, but it's a thin episode plug with no detail. Threadverse passed on this one.
